subject: Re: Problem with alignment
body:
Hi Alister, 

thanks for the fast response! I'll try to solve it as you recommended. 
Thanks again and cheers, 
Albert 

El dia divendres, 1 d’agost del 2025 a les 17:18:09 UTC+2, 
alist...@gmail.com va escriure:

> Hi Albert,
>
> Tilt series alignment can be tricky, we provide automated solutions via 
> wrappers to IMOD/Aretomo but often there is room to improve by 
> - spending time figuring out best parameters for automated solutions
> - making sure bad tilt images are excluded prior to aligning
> - running through things more carefully in the Etomo GUI outside of these 
> wrappers
>
> If you’re learning and only need reconstructions I would recommend 
> generating tilt series stacks with WarpTools ts_stack then aligning them 
> yourself in Etomo following with their tomography guide to understand 
> what’s going on
> IMOD Tomography Guide <https://bio3d.colorado.edu/imod/doc/tomoguide.html>
> bio3d.colorado.edu <https://bio3d.colorado.edu/imod/doc/tomoguide.html>
> <https://bio3d.colorado.edu/imod/doc/tomoguide.html> 
> <https://bio3d.colorado.edu/imod/doc/tomoguide.html>
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Aug 1, 2025, at 03:22, Albert Tell <albertt...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿Hello, 
>
>
> I'm working on some data that only needs straightforward reconstructions. 
> However, I keep getting problems with the final reconstructions, as the 
> structures (especially the grid's edges) appear to be drifting. 
> The mdoc files indicate -174.18 for the tilt axis angle, which after 
> conversion becomes 84.18. This value is very close to the estimates of both 
> WarpTools --do_axis_search as well as etomo values (they both indicate 
> 84.20). 
> For the alignment, I'm using patch tracking and both WarpTools and Etomo 
> give me good results, as the residual error mean and sd are ~0.6 nm.
> Finally, I also tried to fix alignment with Midas in Etomo but I didn't 
> get big improvements in the final reconstructions. 
>
> I'm new in the field of tomography and I'm quite lost on what could be 
> going wrong. 
>
> Thanks in advance! 
> Albert 
>
> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
> "Warp" group.

> .
>
>
